seoTitle: How to Get a 30-Day Free Trial of QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Start a QuickBooks Online free trial without entering a credit card, set up your company profile, and verify your account stays at zero cost.
intro: You can test-drive QuickBooks Online's full feature set for 30 days without committing to a paid plan, as long as you know where to look. This guide walks through finding the free trial option, setting up your company profile, and confirming your account isn't set to bill you automatically.

steps.heading: Visit the QuickBooks Online pricing page
steps.body: Go to the official pricing page and review the available plan tiers before choosing a trial option.

steps.heading: Select the free trial toggle
steps.body: Look for the 'Free 30-Day Trial' toggle switch at the top of the pricing grid and pick your preferred plan tier.

steps.heading: Create your account credentials
steps.body: Enter a valid email address and set a secure password to establish your new Intuit ID.

steps.heading: Complete the onboarding questions
steps.body: Enter your business name and select your industry type as you move through the guided setup screens.

steps.heading: Choose your feature set
steps.body: Toggle on the features you want to test, such as invoicing, expense tracking, or receipt capture.

steps.heading: Verify your trial status
steps.body: Go to the Gear icon, click Account and Settings, then Billing & Subscription to confirm your account shows a Free Trial status and expiration date.

faq.question: Do I need a credit card to start the QuickBooks Online free trial?
faq.answer: No, you can complete the sign-up process without entering payment information.

faq.question: How long does the free trial last?
faq.answer: The trial gives you 30 days of full access before you'd need to choose a paid plan to continue.

faq.question: How do I confirm I'm not being charged during the trial?
faq.answer: Check Account and Settings under Billing & Subscription to make sure your account shows a Free Trial status rather than an active paid plan.

faq.question: Can I explore advanced features during the trial?
faq.answer: Yes, you can toggle on features like invoicing and expense tracking, and even try the Accountant View for a look at advanced ledgers.
